buyer_dependent_items.text: Send Zycus the commercial pricing template over email
Send official confirmation of CFO/CIO meeting date and attendees

competitive_position.summary: Seven-vendor BFSI RFP down to a top 3, with Zycus scored #1 on product and #1 on technology as of 02 Jul (champion Nishan) and named the 'surprise package' over expected big brands like Deloitte. No single rival is buyer-preferred; the strongest CURRENT threat is not a specific logo but the India BFSI implementation-credibility gap, which a shortlisted competitor is exploiting by fielding an ex-IDFC-bank delivery consultant (02 Jul). Among the SF-listed field, GEP Worldwide is the closest size/fit peer. How we win: bring a named India-banking delivery partner to the CFO meeting to close the only red flag, then defend price with a quantified ROI case in the top-3 negotiation.

competitors.name: GEP Worldwide
competitors.source: SF Competitors__c; 7-vendor shortlist per 02 Jul call
competitors.how_we_win: Beat on product and technology (Zycus scored #1 on both); differentiate on Merlin AI automation and single-code integrated platform.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.how_we_win: Position lighter, single-platform integration vs SAP complexity; App Extend for fit without heavy customization.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.how_we_win: Emphasize source-to-pay depth and unlimited-user commercial model the buyer already views favourably (02 Jul).

competitors.name: iValua
competitors.how_we_win: Lead with #1 product/technology scoring and native AI-powered AP automation.

competitors.name: JAGGAER
competitors.how_we_win: Contrast enterprise-grade contract management and banking references across APAC.

competitors.name: Deloitte
competitors.quote: Everyone expected to do, like, Deloitte or those big shots, but you are the surprise package.
competitors.how_we_win: Convert product/technology leadership into an implementation-credibility story via a named India-banking delivery partner.

champion_strength.source: SF OpportunityContactRole; 02 Jul, 23 Jun, 17 Jun Next_Step and calls
champion_strength.summary: Nishan Kumar (DVP - Strategy & Program Management) is a strong, active champion with CFO access. He supplies real-time scoring intel, coaches Zycus on the CFO agenda and the implementation fix, arranged the full evaluation-team demo, and secured the CFO/CIO final meeting. Last contact 02 Jul, no cooling signals. Risk is single-threading: he is currently the only route to power.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Sayantan Mukherjee, Branch Credit Manager
meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: The final decision sits with the CFO (named by the champion as Rajiv Mantry) together with the f&a head and CIO; Nishan stated on 17 Jun that 'your final evaluation will be done with the top management, our CFO... and the CIO.' Zycus has never met the CFO or CIO; the first exposure is the final presentation scheduled for the week of 13 Jul, which Carl Kimball (VP APJ) will lead. A material risk flag exists: the 16 Jun Next_Step records 'Prospect has no budget approval to run this project,' so budget authority is unconfirmed. Sayantan Mukherjee (Branch Credit Manager) is referenced on the 17 Jun demo as the CPO. Move: use the 13 Jul session to open a direct CFO relationship and confirm budget approval.

recommended_moves: Secure a named India-banking delivery partner or consultant for the CFO meeting.
Build the CFO/CIO business deck with implementation plan and ballpark cost estimate.
Deliver the CFO and CIO final presentation in person and open a direct EB relationship.
